title: Browser Storage Services
description: SSR-safe wrappers for localStorage, sessionStorage, and cookies: JSON persistence, optional AES encryption for localStorage, and cookie management with SameSite and secure flags.
imports: import { AXLocalStorageService, AXSessionStorageService, AXCookieStorageService, AX_LOCALSTORAGE_SECRET_KEY, AXStorage, AXCookieOptions } from '@acorex/core/storage';

hints.title: Provide services explicitly
hints.content: Storage services are not providedIn: 'root'. Register AXLocalStorageService, AXSessionStorageService, and/or AXCookieStorageService in app.config.ts or component providers.

hints.title: LocalStorage secret provider
hints.content: AXLocalStorageService injects AX_LOCALSTORAGE_SECRET_KEY when the service is created. Provide it whenever you register the local storage service, especially before calling setWithEncryption() / getWithEncription().

hints.title: Browser only
hints.content: All methods no-op or return null during SSR. Values are automatically JSON-serialized for local/session storage.

hints.title: Method name typo
hints.content: The decrypt method is spelled getWithEncription (missing 'y') in the source — use this exact name when calling the API.

types.title: AXStorage
types.description: Common interface implemented by local and session storage services.

types.title: AXLocalStorageService
types.description: Persistent key-value storage with JSON and optional AES encryption.

methods.content:
/**
 * Reads and JSON.parses a value. Returns null if missing or on server.
 */
get<T>(key: string): T | null

/**
 * JSON.stringify + localStorage.setItem.
 */
set<T>(key: string, value: T): void

/**
 * AES-encrypt JSON value before storing.
 * Requires AX_LOCALSTORAGE_SECRET_KEY.
 */
setWithEncryption<T>(key: string, value: T): void

/**
 * Decrypt and JSON.parse stored value.
 * Note: method name is getWithEncription in source.
 */
getWithEncription<T>(key: string): T | null

types.title: AXSessionStorageService
types.description: Tab-scoped storage with the same JSON API as localStorage.

types.title: AXCookieStorageService
types.description: Document.cookie wrapper with encode/decode and SameSite support.

methods.content: set(name: string, value: string, options?: AXCookieOptions): void
delete(name: string, path?, domain?, secure?, sameSite?): void

props.name: expires?
props.description: Number = days from now, or an absolute Date.

props.name: secure?
props.description: Secure flag. Forced true when sameSite is 'None'.

types.title: AX_LOCALSTORAGE_SECRET_KEY
types.description: InjectionToken<string> — AES passphrase for encrypted localStorage entries.

content.data:
The @acorex/core/storage package offers three browser storage adapters:

• AXLocalStorageService — survives browser restarts, JSON values, optional AES encryption
• AXSessionStorageService — cleared when the tab closes, same JSON API
• AXCookieStorageService — string cookies with path, domain, expiry, secure, and SameSite

All services guard against SSR using isBrowser() or isPlatformBrowser(). They do not auto-register — you must add them to your app providers.

sections.title: Basic Usage
sections.description: Register the services once, then inject the storage adapters to persist JSON values, tab state, and cookies.

sections.title: Encrypted LocalStorage
sections.description: Store sensitive data with AES encryption.

sections.title: Cookie Management
sections.description: Set, read, and delete cookies with security options.

sections.title: Real-World: Layout Preferences
sections.description: How LayoutService persists locale and theme.

content.data:
| Service | Scope | Data type | Best for |
|---------|-------|-----------|----------|
| AXLocalStorageService | Persistent across sessions | JSON objects | User prefs, locale, theme, cached settings |
| AXSessionStorageService | Current tab only | JSON objects | Multi-step forms, temporary wizard state |
| AXCookieStorageService | Configurable expiry | Plain strings | Server-readable tokens, analytics IDs, SSR-friendly flags |

Use setWithEncryption for obfuscating local values (not for high-security secrets — prefer httpOnly cookies from the server).

All JSON storage methods return null on the server — always handle null when restoring state on init.
